data.question: Does this ECG show ST elevation in at least two of the inferior leads, including leads II, III, and aVF?

Does this ECG show pathological Q waves in at least two of the inferior leads, including leads II, III, and aVF? Note that a pathological Q wave in these leads is defined as (1) a Q wave with a duration greater than 30 milliseconds and an amplitude greater than one fifth of the following R amplitude, or (2) a deep S wave with only a small R wave preceding it.
Which of the following leads show the pathological Q waves? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complex with pathological Q wave? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG strip, provided in seconds.
